children.text: the use, storage, and disposal of agrichemicals by users is prudent, economical, and environmentally sound; and
agrichemical users, dealers, and the general public understand the implications of their actions and the potential effects on water.

children.heading: Effect on existing authority
children.label: b Effect on existing authority
children.text: The authority granted in subsection (a) does not alter or effect the responsibility of the Environmental Protection Agency under the Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 136

children.heading: Participation
children.label: c Participation
children.text: The following agencies shall participate in the Department’s water program: the Agricultural Research Service; the Agricultural Stabilization and Conservation Service; the Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service; the National Institute of Food and Agriculture, in conjunction with the system of State agricultural experiment stations and State and county cooperative extension services; the Economic Research Service; the Forest Service; the National Agricultural Library; the National Agricultural Statistics Service; the Soil Conservation Service; and other agencies within the Department deemed appropriate by the Secretary.
